content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and

79	/-- **Sub-task (b) range containment (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the topological image of
80	the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` lies inside the open image of the chart-1 affine open
81	`Proj.awayι (X 1) : Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`.
82	
83	**Mathematical content.** `onePt.left = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 (evalIntoGlobal v) _`
84	with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`. Since `evalIntoGlobal v (X 1) = (ΓSpecIso k̄).inv 1 = 1` is a unit in
85	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`, the preimage of `D₊(X 1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` under `onePt.left` is
86	`(Spec k̄).basicOpen 1 = ⊤`, so the image of `onePt.left` lies entirely in
87	`Set.range (Proj.awayι (X 1)) = D₊(X 1)`.
88	
89	The range chain (iter-184 closure):
90	(i) `Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange` + `Proj.opensRange_awayι` rewrite the RHS into
91	`↑(Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X 1))`; (ii) pointwise destructure to `onePt.left x ∈ basicOpen`;
92	(iii) `Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage` + `change` expose `onePt.left = fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 φ hφ`
93	(`pointOfVec` unfolds through `Over.homMk` even though `evalIntoGlobal`/`pointOfVec` are
94	private — defeq is unaffected by name visibility); (iv) `fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen`
95	reduces to `(Spec k̄).basicOpen ((ΓSpecIso k̄).inv.hom (eval (·=1) (X 1)))`;
96	(v) `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit` + `simp` closes via `eval_X` + `map_one` + `isUnit_one`.
97	Axiom-clean: `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
98	
99	Iter-190: extracted from the previous `∃`-packed `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of
100	the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E `projappiso` analysis) so the chart-1 lift
101	`iotaGm_r_1` is exposed as a named `noncomputable def`. Downstream
102	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can then apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
103	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` without re-deriving the range witness via `cancel_mono`. -/
104	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_range_subset (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
105	    Set.range ⇑(ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⊆
106	      Set.range ⇑(Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
108	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
109	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
110	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)) := by
111	  -- Iter-190 prover closure (point-witness reshape). The iter-184 closed body relied on
112	  -- `rw [← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]` to expose the RHS `Set.range` as a `(...).opensRange`
113	  -- coercion; in the standalone signature here, elaboration types the LHS at
114	  -- `Set ↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` whereas `Proj.awayι _ _ _ _` is typed at
115	  -- `Set ↥(Proj 𝒜)`, so the rewrite pattern does not match. We sidestep this by
116	  -- destructuring point-wise FIRST, then `change`-ing the membership goal into
117	  -- `(...).opensRange.1` (a defeq reshape that works because the underlying carriers
118	  -- `↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and `↥(Proj 𝒜)` are defeq through
119	  -- `Scheme.asOver` / `OverClass.asOver`). The remainder of the chain is identical to
120	  -- the iter-184 body.
121	  rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
122	  change (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left.base x ∈ (Proj.awayι _ _ _ _).opensRange.1
123	  rw [Proj.opensRange_awayι]
124	  change x ∈ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen _ _
125	  change x ∈ Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ ⁻¹ᵁ _
126	  rw [Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen _ _ _
127	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
128	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)]
129	  refine (Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ ?_).symm ▸ TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x
130	  change IsUnit ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
131	    ((MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Fin 2 => (1 : kbar)))
132	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
133	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)))
134	  simp

139	`IsOpenImmersion.lift` of the open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)` together with the

145	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
146	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` instead of re-deriving the lift identity via `cancel_mono`. -/

148	    Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
149	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away

179	    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) ≫
180	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =

187	`s : Gm.left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover).X 1`, built as the `pullback.lift` over
188	`pullback q (awayι (![X 0, X 1] 1))` with the iotaGm-prefix as `q`-component and
189	`Gm.hom ≫ r_1` as `awayι`-component. -/
190	private noncomputable def iotaGm_chart1_section (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
191	    (r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
192	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
193	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
194	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
195	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))))
196	    (h_r_1 : r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
197	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :

199	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)

201	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) :
202	    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2) :=
203	  pullback.lift
204	    (pullback.lift
205	      ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left)
206	      (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left)
207	      (iotaGm_inner_lift_compat kbar))
208	    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ r_1)

211	      rfl)

213	/-- **Sub-task (f) helper (iter-183 Lane E):** the section `s` (built from the chart-1
214	factorisation `r_1` of `onePt.left`) composed with `gmScalingP1_chart 1` is an open
215	immersion `Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`.
216	
217	**Mathematical content.** The composition realises the canonical inclusion
218	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` sending `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`. Decomposes as
219	
220	```
221	Gm.left = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]
222	   ↪ Spec k̄[u]               (localization at `t`, via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`)
223	   ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))     (via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.symm`, an iso)
224	   ↪ ℙ¹.left                  (via `Proj.awayι (X 1)`).

228

230	factorisation as an explicit witness (`Spec.map (algMap MvPoly Unit kbar GmRing kbar) ≫
231	Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom ≫ Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1) …`),

243	Proj.awayι (X 1)` and the pullback-lift section's projections via `pullback.lift_fst`/
244	`pullback.lift_snd`; the cover-iso chain (`pullbackSymmetry ≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso
245	≫ pullback.congrHom ≫ pullbackSpecIso`) and the chart-ring map's `eval₂Hom ∘ iso`

249	`Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`) is **sidestepped** via a `change` step that

260	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`). iter-186+ closure target. -/
261	private lemma iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion [IsAlgClosed kbar]
262	    (r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
263	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
264	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
265	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
266	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))))
267	    (h_r_1 : r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
268	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
269	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
270	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
271	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1) =
272	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) :
273	    IsOpenImmersion (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar r_1 h_r_1
274	      ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2)) := by
275	  -- The composition equals the canonical inclusion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] → ℙ¹` sending
276	  -- `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`, which factors as three open immersions:
277	  --   Spec(GmRing) → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) → ℙ¹.
278	  -- The first is `Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))`, an open
279	  -- immersion via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization` (Mathlib).
280	  -- The second is `Spec.map homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.toRingHom`, an iso (hence
281	  -- open immersion).
282	  -- The third is `Proj.awayι (X 1)`, an open immersion (Mathlib instance).
283	  -- Step 1: the canonical 3-step open immersion is set up explicitly.
284	  -- `Gm.left → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar)` via the localization algebraMap.
285	  haveI h_loc : IsOpenImmersion
286	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
287	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)))) :=
288	    IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization
289	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
290	  -- `Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 X_1)` via the chart-ring iso.
291	  -- `RingEquiv` lifts to a `CommRingCat` iso whose `.hom = CommRingCat.ofHom (toRingHom)`,
292	  -- so the latter is `IsIso`, and `Spec.map` preserves isos.
293	  haveI h_iso_isIso : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
294	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) := by
295	    rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom
296	          = (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.hom from rfl]
297	    exact (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.isIso_hom
298	  haveI h_iso : IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
299	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom)) :=
300	    inferInstance
301	  -- Reduce to the substantive equation: `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
302	  -- Once established, the conclusion follows from `IsOpenImmersion.comp`
303	  -- applied three times (one per factor of the canonical chain).
304	  haveI h_proj : IsOpenImmersion (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
305	      (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
306	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos) :=
307	    inferInstance
308	  suffices h_eq : iotaGm_chart1_section kbar r_1 h_r_1
309	        ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) =
310	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
311	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
312	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
313	          (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) ≫
314	          Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
315	            (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
316	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos by
317	    rw [h_eq]
318	    -- Composition of three open immersions is an open immersion.
319	    exact @IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_loc
320	      (@IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_iso h_proj)
321	  -- Prove the equation `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
322	  -- Stage 1: Fuse the two trailing `Spec.map`s on the RHS into a single `Spec.map`
323	  --   via `Spec.map_comp` + `CommRingCat.ofHom_comp`, so both sides end with
324	  --   `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f_ring) ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` where
325	  --   `f_ring = (algMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
326	  --             (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`.
327	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- Stage 2: Unfold `gmScalingP1_chart` on the LHS and re-associate so both
329	  --   sides end with `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Apply `congr 1` to cancel the
330	  --   trailing `Proj.awayι (X 1)` factor (open immersion ⟹ mono, but `congr 1`
331	  --   is structural and avoids needing the mono instance directly).
332	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
333	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
334	  congr 1
335	  -- Residual goal: identify the LHS prefix (section ≫ cover_X_iso 1).hom ≫
336	  -- Spec.map (eval₂Hom_chart1_ringMap ∘ iso.toRingHom)) with the RHS prefix
337	  -- `Spec.map(f_ring) : Spec(GmRing) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))`.
338	  -- Both sides go between affine schemes. The RHS is a single `Spec.map`;

590	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
591	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
592	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
593	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
594	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
595	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
596	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
597	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
598	  have hcollapse :
599	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
600	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
601	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
602	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
603	    have hreshape :
604	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
605	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
606	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
607	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
608	      congr 1
609	      rw [← Category.assoc,
610	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
611	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
612	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
613	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
614	  have hcorhyp :
615	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
616	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
617	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
618	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
619	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
620	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
621	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
622	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
623	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
624	  have hfW :
625	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
626	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
627	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]

629	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
630	  have hSndFW :
631	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
632	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
633	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))

635	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
636	    congr 1
637	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
638	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
639	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
640	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.

642	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)

646	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-\(0\) curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	label{chap:avr_for_rr}
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
10	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
11	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
12	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
13	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
14	% keystone directly). The headline prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
15	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
16	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
17	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
18	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\(, hence \)df = 0\(, hence \)f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\(, \)(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\( (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \)0$ and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\( (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \)f(0) = \eta_A\( and composing \)h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\(, one applies additivity of \)\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
45	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).
78	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
79	\begin{lemma}
80	\leanok
81	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
82	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
83	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
84	  
85	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
86	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
87	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
88	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
89	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
90	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
91	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
92	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
93	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety, and let \(Y\), \(Z\) be any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
94	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism such that for some \(y_0 \in Y\) the image
95	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0 \in Z\). Then there is a morphism
96	  \(g \colon Y \to Z\) with \(f = g \circ p_2\), where \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
97	  projection; that is, \(f\) depends only on the \(Y\)-coordinate, and \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) for any
98	  fixed \(x_0 \in X\).
99	\end{lemma}
100	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
101	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
102	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
103	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
104	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
105	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
106	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
107	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
108	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
109	% assertion."
110	\begin{proof}
111	\leanok
112	% NOTE (iter-159 review): the iter-157 unsoundness REMAINS REPAIRED. The collapse hypothesis
113	% (_hf : f(X x {y0}) = {z0}) is threaded through all three helpers (rigidity_lemma ->
114	% rigidity_core -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open) and GENUINELY CONSUMED in the body of
115	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open (proves y0 \notin G, the load-bearing non-emptiness of V := Y - G).
116	% The counterexample f = fst is excluded (under f = fst, _hf forces X to collapse to a point).
117	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open is sorry-free in its OWN body (hfib, the pullback-fibre fact over the
118	% k-bar-point y0, is closed). rigidity_snd_lift and snd_left_isClosedMap are axiom-clean (no sorryAx).
119	% NOTE (iter-160): the \uses edges run FORWARD along the true Lean dependency order
120	% (rigidity_lemma -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open -> rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine), so the
121	% not-proven status of the residual sorry propagates up and de-launders BOTH thm:rigidity_lemma and
122	% lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open. No backward edge from the leaf lemmas back up the chain.
123	% NOTE (iter-161): bridge 2 (rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine) was DECOMPOSED into two named
124	% top-level sub-lemmas along the route-B (cohomology-free) split:
125	%   - Step 2, morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints: "two morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a
126	%     reduced Jacobson source into a separated target are equal" -- PROVEN axiom-clean (the
127	%     dense-closed-points coproduct probe + ext_of_isDominant; the connective Mathlib lacks).
128	%   - Step 1, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine: Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy
129	%     -- the genuinely-deep residual, still `sorry`. THIS is now the chain's lone deep residual
130	%     (bridge 2's body is real assembly: Step 2 proven, the JacobsonSpace U instance now derivable).
131	% SIGNATURE CHANGE (iter-161, plan-authorized): Step 2's globalisation needs the closed points of
132	% the saturated open U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson, which over Spec k-bar follows from (X tensor Y)
133	% being LOCALLY OF FINITE TYPE. So [LocallyOfFiniteType (X tensor Y).hom] is now carried on FIVE
134	% chain lemmas (rigidity_lemma, rigidity_core, rigidity_eqOn_dense_open,
135	% rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine), in
136	% addition to [IsAlgClosed kbar]. Free downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type
137	% over k-bar. The earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim is hereby retired.
138	  Fix any point \(x_0 \in X\) and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) (the
139	  composite of \(y \mapsto (x_0, y)\) with \(f\)). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety (in
140	  particular reduced and irreducible, so separated and with no embedded structure), to prove
141	  the equality of morphisms \(f = g \circ p_2\) it suffices to prove they agree on a non-empty
142	  open subset of \(X \times Y\).
143	  Let \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U\) (a closed set),
144	  and set \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection
145	  \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a \emph{closed} map (this is the defining property of
146	  completeness/properness); hence \(G\), the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\), is closed in
147	  \(Y\). The hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U\) gives
148	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), so \(y_0 \notin G\). Therefore
149	  \(V := Y - G\) is a non-empty open subset of \(Y\).
150	  For each \(y \in V\) we have \(X \times \{y\} \cap f^{-1}(F) = \varnothing\), i.e.
151	  \(f(X \times \{y\}) \subseteq U\). Thus the complete (proper) variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is
152	  mapped by \(f\) into the \emph{affine} variety \(U\). A proper connected variety mapping to an
153	  affine variety has image a single point (a global regular function on a complete connected
154	  variety is constant). Hence \(f(X \times \{y\})\) is one point, necessarily
155	  \(f(x_0, y) = g(y)\). So for all \(x \in X\) and \(y \in V\),
156	  \[ f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g(y) = (g \circ p_2)(x, y). \]
157	  The two morphisms \(f\) and \(g \circ p_2\) thus agree on the non-empty open
158	  \(X \times V\), which is dense in the irreducible \(X \times Y\); as \(Z\) is separated they
159	  agree everywhere. \qedhere
160	\end{proof}
161	\begin{remark}
162	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_cube_free}
163	  The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} uses \emph{only} (i) that completeness of \(X\) makes
164	  \(p_2\) a closed map, and (ii) that a proper connected variety has no nonconstant map to an
165	  affine variety. It uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, and is valid in
166	  arbitrary characteristic. This is the designated formalisation entry point: it is the
167	  lowest-prerequisite link of the chain. (Mumford gives this as the geometric heart of the
168	  ``second proof'' of commutativity of an abelian variety; Milne packages the same statement
169	  as the Rigidity Theorem~1.1, requiring \(V\) complete and \(V \times W\) geometrically
170	  irreducible.)
171	\end{remark}
172	\begin{remark}
173	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_decomposition}
174	  The Lean formalisation of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} splits into three helper steps, mirroring
175	  the proof above: \texttt{rigidity\_snd\_lift} (the cartesian-monoidal algebra identifying the
176	  collapsed map \((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\) followed by \(f\) with \(g \circ p_2\));
177	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} (the scheme-level gluing: two maps out of the geometrically irreducible
178	  reduced \(X \times Y\) into the separated \(Z\) that agree on a non-empty open subset agree
179	  everywhere, by separatedness); and \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open}
180	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}, the genuine geometry: the construction of the non-empty
181	  open \(X \times V\) and the slice-constancy of \(f\) on it). The collapse hypothesis
182	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\) must be threaded through \emph{all three} helpers, since it is
183	  what makes \(V\) non-empty; a decomposition that drops it from the lower helpers is unsound
184	  (see \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}).
185	  \emph{Status (iter-161).} Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) is \textbf{built} as the axiom-clean
186	  helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap}. The fibre-over-\(y_0\) fact is \textbf{closed} (via the
187	  coarse \texttt{PullbackCarrier} layer), so \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open} is
188	  \texttt{sorry}-free in its own body. Bridge~2 (slice-constancy on the saturated open \(U\),
189	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}) has now been \textbf{decomposed} along its
190	  cohomology-free route-(c) split into two named sub-lemmas: the globalisation
191	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2, ``agree at every closed point of a reduced
192	  Jacobson source \(\Rightarrow\) equal'') is \textbf{proven axiom-clean}, and the per-closed-slice
193	  constancy \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine} (Step~1, Mumford's slice
194	  argument) is the chain's \emph{single genuinely-deep residual} \texttt{sorry}. The body of
195	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} is therefore real assembly (Step~2 over Step~1).
196	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) STALE as of iter-162 — Step~1 is now PROVEN and the WHOLE
197	  % Rigidity-Lemma chain (thm:rigidity_lemma … lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine,
198	  % via the new helper lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral) is sorry-free + axiom-clean
199	  % ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, re-verified). The "single genuinely-deep residual
200	  % sorry" wording here and in the proof blocks of lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open / …saturated… /
201	  % …eqAt_closedPoint… should be refreshed by the plan/blueprint-writer to "chain closed iter-162".
202	  This route requires the base field to be algebraically closed \emph{and} the source to be of finite
203	  type: Step~2 needs the closed points of \(U\) to be dense (the Jacobson-space property), which over
204	  the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) holds because \(X \times Y\) is locally of finite type.

206	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) across the chain lemmas
207	  (\texttt{rigidity\_lemma}, \texttt{rigidity\_core}, \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open},
208	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_saturated\_open\_to\_affine},
209	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqAt\_closedPoint\_of\_proper\_into\_affine}); the earlier claim that
210	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) is the \emph{only} added instance is retired. Both cost nothing
211	  downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\),
212	  and the consumers already assume it. The relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
213	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a genuine Mathlib cohomology gap and is \emph{deliberately
