216	\begin{lemma}
217	\leanok
218	[Dense-open agreement: the geometric heart of the Rigidity Lemma]
219	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
221	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
222	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
223	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
224	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
225	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
226	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
227	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
228	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
229	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
230	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
231	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
232	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
233	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
234	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
235	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
236	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
237	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
238	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\( is the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
239	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
240	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
241	  \medskip
242	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
243	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
244	  \subseteq U_0\( keeps the slice over \)y_0\( inside the affine \)U_0\(, away from \)F = Z - U_0$. A
245	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
246	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
247	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
248	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
249	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
250	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
251	  \medskip
252	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
253	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
254	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
255	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
256	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
257	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the
258	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
259	  closed points of the saturated open \(U\) to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ \(U\) to be a Jacobson space.
260	  Over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) this holds precisely because \(X \times Y\) is \emph{locally of
261	  finite type} over \(\bar k\) (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
262	  property of \(\Spec \bar k\), and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
263	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\). Accordingly the formalization carries
264	  \emph{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
265	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) on this lemma (in addition to
266	  \([\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]\)), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
267	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
268	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
269	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
270	  consumers \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
271	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
272	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) --- the base-field variable is literally named \(\mathtt{kbar}\) ---
273	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over \(\bar k\), so the
274	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
275	\end{lemma}
276	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
277	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
278	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
279	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
280	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
281	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
282	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
283	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
284	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
285	% assertion."
286	\begin{proof}
287	  \leanok
288	  Let \(U_0\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U_0\), and set
289	  \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a
290	  \emph{closed} map, so \(G\) (the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\)) is closed in \(Y\). The
291	  collapse hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U_0\) gives
292	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), hence \(y_0 \notin G\); this is the only place
293	  the collapse hypothesis is used, and it is essential, for \(V := Y - G\) is non-empty precisely
294	  because \(y_0 \in V\). For each \(y \in V\) the proper variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is mapped by \(f\)
295	  into the affine \(U_0\), hence to a single point, necessarily \(f(x_0, y)\). So for all \(x \in X\),
296	  \(y \in V\), we have \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x, y)\), i.e.\ \(f\) and
297	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on the non-empty open \(U := X \times V\). (In the Lean
298	  encoding the two slice-constancy bridges are the closed-map step from properness and the
299	  ``proper-into-affine has single-point image'' step; see the docstring of
300	  \texttt{rigidity\_core}.)
301	  \medskip
302	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization notes (the concrete char-free Mathlib route).} The textbook
303	  proof above is realised in Lean by three pieces. They cite Mathlib lemma names as the chosen
304	  route; none uses cohomology, and the relative Stein-factorisation framing is deliberately
305	  \emph{avoided} (see below).
306	  \emph{Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) --- BUILT.} ``\(X\) complete \(\Rightarrow p_2\) closed'' is
307	  the axiom-clean helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap} (iter-158). It is obtained from
308	  \texttt{IsProper.toUniversallyClosed} on \(X\), base-changed across the canonical pullback square
309	  to make \(p_2\) universally closed, using that the monoidal projection \emph{is} the chosen
310	  pullback projection: \texttt{Over.snd\_left} rewrites \((\mathtt{snd}\,X\,Y).\mathtt{left}\) to
311	  \(\mathtt{Limits.pullback.snd}\ X.\mathtt{hom}\ Y.\mathtt{hom}\) exactly. This step is already
312	  proven.
313	  \emph{The fibre fact (non-emptiness of \(V\)) --- RESOLVED route, char-free.} That the slice
314	  \(X \times \{y_0\}\) over the \(\bar k\)-rational point \(y_0\) is exactly the image of the slice
315	  section \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) is formalised \emph{without} the residue-field /
316	  \texttt{Triplet} / tensor-product machinery (that fine \texttt{carrierEquiv} layer is the wrong
317	  granularity and is to be avoided). One pastes the identity square for the section against the
318	  canonical pullback square (\texttt{IsPullback.of\_right},
319	  \texttt{IsPullback.of\_horiz\_isIso}; the section identities \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1\) and
320	  \(s \fatsemi p_2 = X.\mathtt{hom} \fatsemi y_0\) come from \texttt{lift\_fst}/\texttt{lift\_snd}
321	  with \texttt{Over.w} on the unit) and reads off the fibre with the \emph{coarse} topological
322	  layer of \texttt{PullbackCarrier}, namely
323	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pullback.image\_preimage\_eq\_of\_isPullback}. This needs no
324	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}]\).
325	  \emph{Bridge~2 (slice-constancy / the agreement equation) --- RESOLVED route, cohomology-FREE.}
326	  ``Proper connected slice into affine \(\Rightarrow\) single point, glued to a morphism equality on
327	  \(U\)'' is realised by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route, \emph{not} by any
328	  relative Stein-factorisation or proper-pushforward \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) statement: the
329	  latter is a genuine Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no \(H^0\) base change), and route~(c)
330	  exists precisely to avoid coherent cohomology, so it must \emph{not} be attempted. The concrete
331	  stack is: for each closed point \(y \in V\) one has \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\) (alg-closed, finite type);
332	  the slice \(X_y\) is proper integral, so its global sections form a field
333	  (\texttt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}) module-finite over \(\bar k\)
334	  (\texttt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}), and algebraic closedness kills the finite
335	  extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring
336	  map on global sections (\texttt{ext\_of\_isAffine}), so \(f\) and
337	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on each closed slice. Closed points are dense in the
338	  locally-of-finite-type \(U\) (\texttt{closure\_closedPoints} / the Jacobson-space property of
339	  locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-schemes; in the formalization this density is supplied by the new
340	  hypothesis \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)), so the per-slice
341	  agreement globalises to all of \(U\) via the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
342	  \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (the same handle \texttt{rigidity\_core} already
343	  uses). The packaged ``morphisms agreeing on a dense set of closed points are equal''
344	  hom-extensionality --- the one connective Mathlib does not supply directly (it gives only the
345	  single-dominant-morphism \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}) --- has now been built as the axiom-clean
346	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2): it assembles the closed points into one
347	  dominant coproduct probe and applies \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}. The whole argument is extracted
348	  into the dedicated obligation \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} below, whose body
349	  wires that proven Step~2 over the per-slice Step~1
350	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}); Step~1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice
351	  constancy) is now the chain's \emph{single} genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
352	\end{proof}
353	\begin{lemma}
354	\leanok
355	[Bridge~2: slice-constancy on a saturated open mapping into an affine]
356	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
357	  
358	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
359	  % NOTE (iter-161): the iter-160 SIGNATURE GAP is RESOLVED. The Lean proof wires Step 2
360	  % (morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, PROVEN axiom-clean) over Step 1
361	  % (rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, the residual sorry). Step 2 needs the closed
362	  % points of U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson; over k̄ this follows from (X⊗Y) being locally of finite
363	  % type, so [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom] is now a stated hypothesis of this lemma (and across the
364	  % chain). With it, the `JacobsonSpace U` instance is a routine discharge (LocallyOfFiniteType.
365	  % jacobsonSpace + JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding), no longer an as-typed gap. The only `sorry`
366	  % reachable from this lemma is now Step 1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy). The hypothesis
367	  % set is recorded in the statement and proof prose below (both [IsAlgClosed k̄] AND
368	  % [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom]); the earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim
369	  % is retired.
370	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
371	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
372	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
373	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
374	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
375	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
376	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
377	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(X\), \(Y\), \(Z\) be objects of
378	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) with \(X\) proper, \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced,
379	  and \emph{locally of finite type} over \(\bar k\) (the formalization carries
380	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\), which makes the saturated open \(U\)
381	  below a Jacobson space, so its closed points are dense --- the hypothesis Step~2 of the proof
382	  consumes), and \(Z\) separated. Let \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a \(\bar k\)-point
383	  \(x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X\), and write $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
384	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\( for the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$''
385	  endomorphism. Suppose:
386	  \begin{itemize}
387	    \item \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) is a \(p_2\)-\emph{saturated} open, i.e.\ \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\)
388	      for a set \(V_{\mathrm{set}} \subseteq Y\) (so \(U\) contains the whole fibre \(X_y\) over each of
389	      its points);
390	    \item \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) is an \emph{affine} open;
391	    \item \(f(U) \subseteq U_0\), i.e.\ \(f\) maps \(U\) into the affine \(U_0\).
392	  \end{itemize}
393	  Then \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on \(U\) as scheme morphisms:
394	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\).
395	\end{lemma}
396	\begin{proof}
397	  \leanok
398	  This is the formalization realization of Mumford's step ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete
399	  variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single
400	  point of \(U\)''. We realise it by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route that is
401	  \emph{cohomology-free}; in particular the relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
402	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no
403	  \(H^0\) base change) and is \emph{deliberately avoided}. The argument splits into two named
404	  sub-lemmas, one residual and one proven.
405	  \medskip
406	  \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-closed-slice constancy) --- \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}, the residual.}
407	  Fix a closed point \(x\) of \(U\), lying over a closed point \(y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}\).
408	  Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(Y\) is of finite type over \(\bar k\), the residue field
409	  is \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\). Because \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\) is saturated, \(U\) contains the
410	  \emph{entire} fibre \(X_y \cong X\) over \(y\); hence \(f\) maps the proper integral slice \(X_y\) into the
411	  affine \(U_0\). By \(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\) the global sections \(\Gamma(X_y)\) form a
412	  field, module-finite over \(\bar k\) by \(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\), and
413	  algebraic closedness collapses the finite extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into
414	  the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring map on global sections (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), so the
415	  restriction of \(f\) to \(X_y\) factors through a single \(\bar k\)-point of \(U_0\) --- necessarily the
416	  point \(f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x)\). Thus \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\)
417	  agree (after the residue-field probe) at every closed point of \(U\). This per-slice statement is the
418	  chain's single genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
419	  \medskip
420	  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (globalisation over dense closed points) --- \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, proven.}
421	  The closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-scheme \(U\)
422	  (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\) / the Jacobson-space property, supplied here by the new hypothesis
423	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)). To turn ``\(f\) and
424	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree at each closed point'' into a single morphism equality on \(U\),
425	  the proven globalisation \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} assembles the closed points
426	  into one dominant probe --- the coproduct
427	  \(\coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,U} \Spec \kappa(x) \to U\), whose range is dense --- and feeds
428	  it to the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity handle \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (the same
429	  family \(\mathtt{rigidity\_core}\) uses). This yields the morphism equality
430	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\) on all of \(U\). The
431	  ``agrees on a dense set of closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-equality'' connective is the one piece
432	  Mathlib does not package directly (it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
433	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\)); that bespoke build is now done in
434	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, and it is \emph{not} cohomology. \qedhere
435	\end{proof}
436	\begin{lemma}\leanok
437	[Step~2: morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a Jacobson source are equal]
438	  \label{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
439	  
440	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
441	  % Project-bespoke assembly: NO external source. Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
442	  % ext_of_isDominant; the "dense closed points => hom-extensionality" connective is built here.
443	  Let \(W\), \(Z\) be schemes with \(W\) \emph{reduced}, \(W\) a \emph{Jacobson space}, and \(Z\)
444	  \emph{separated}. Let \(g_1, g_2 \colon W \to Z\) be morphisms. If, for every \emph{closed} point
445	  \(x \in W\), the two morphisms agree after restriction along the residue-field point
446	  \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to W\) (in Lean, $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 =
447	  W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\(), then \)g_1 = g_2$.
448	\end{lemma}
449	\begin{proof}
450	\leanok
451	  This is the ``dense closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-extensionality'' connective that Mathlib does
452	  not package directly: it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism extensionality
453	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (a dominant morphism into a separated target is an epimorphism for
454	  the purpose of right-cancellation). We assemble the per-closed-point hypotheses into one such
455	  dominant probe.
456	  Form the coproduct \(P := \coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} \Spec \kappa(x)\) and the morphism
457	  \(\mathrm{probe} \colon P \to W\) defined by \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\) of the residue-field points
458	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\). Its topological range contains every closed point of \(W\):
459	  the inclusion \(\iota_x \colon \Spec \kappa(x) \to P\) composes with \(\mathrm{probe}\) to
460	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) (the defining property of \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\)), and the
461	  range of \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) is the singleton \(\{x\}\)
462	  (\(\mathtt{Scheme.range\_fromSpecResidueField}\)). Because \(W\) is a Jacobson space, the closed points
463	  are dense (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\), i.e.\ \(\overline{\mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} = W\)), so
464	  the range of \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dense and \(\mathrm{probe}\) is \emph{dominant}.
465	  Componentwise, \(g_1\) and \(g_2\) agree after \(\mathrm{probe}\): precomposing with each coproduct
466	  inclusion \(\iota_x\) reduces (by \(\mathtt{Sigma.\iota\_desc}\)) to
467	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 = W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\),
468	  which is the hypothesis at the closed point \(x\). By the universal property of the coproduct
469	  (\(\mathtt{Sigma.hom\_ext}\)) this gives \(\mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_1 = \mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_2\).
470	  Since \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dominant and \(Z\) is separated, \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) cancels it
471	  on the left to yield \(g_1 = g_2\).
472	\end{proof}
473	\begin{lemma}
474	\leanok
475	[Global-sections constancy: two \(\bar k\)-points of a proper integral source into an affine agree]
476	  \label{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
478	  % Project-bespoke assembly of Mathlib lemmas: NO external source. Realises, cohomology-free, the
479	  % classical "a global regular function on a proper integral variety over an algebraically closed
480	  % field is constant" --- precisely the affine-image step of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma proof quoted
481	  % above (the "complete slice into an affine maps to a single point" sentence, Ch.~II \S4, p.~43).
482	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(W\) be an integral scheme equipped with a structure
483	  morphism \(w \colon W \to \Spec \bar k\) that is universally closed (proper) and locally of finite
484	  type. Let \(g \colon W \to V\) be a morphism into an \emph{affine} scheme \(V\). Then any two
485	  \(\bar k\)-points of \(W\) --- i.e.\ any two sections \(a, b \colon \Spec \bar k \to W\) of \(w\) (so
486	  \(a \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\) and \(b \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\)) --- satisfy \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\).
487	  \medskip
488	  \noindent\textbf{Every hypothesis is load-bearing} (confirmed by the iter-161 audit).
489	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) collapses the finite extension \(\Gamma(W)/\bar k\); integrality of
490	  \(W\) makes \(\Gamma(W)\) a domain (without it a two-point disjoint union is a counterexample);
491	  \(\mathtt{UniversallyClosed}\) is what upgrades \(\Gamma(W)\) to a \emph{field} (without it
492	  \(\mathbb A^1\), with \(\Gamma = \bar k[t]\) and two distinct \(\bar k\)-points, is a counterexample);
493	  \(\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\) gives finiteness of \(\Gamma(W)\) over \(\bar k\); and
494	  \([\mathtt{IsAffine}\ V]\) is what lets \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\) pin the morphism by its ring map
495	  on global sections.
496	\end{lemma}
497	\begin{proof}
498	\leanok
499	  Because \(W\) is integral and \(w\) is universally closed, the global sections
500	  \(\Gamma(W, \mathcal O_W)\) form a field (\(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\)), and the structure
501	  ring map \(w^\sharp \colon \bar k \to \Gamma(W)\) (the map \(\mathtt{wk.appTop}\) after the canonical
502	  identification \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k) \cong \bar k\)) is integral / module-finite
503	  (\(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\)). Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and
504	  \(\Gamma(W)\) is a domain finite over it, that map is bijective
505	  (\(\mathtt{IsAlgClosed.ringHom\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}\)); thus \(\Gamma(W) = \bar k\) and
506	  \(w^\sharp\) is an isomorphism on global sections --- in particular an epimorphism. Both sections
507	  \(a\), \(b\) left-invert \(w^\sharp\) on global sections (they are sections of \(w\)), so they induce the
508	  \emph{same} ring map \(a^\sharp = b^\sharp\) on \(\Gamma(W)\) (cancel the epimorphism \(w^\sharp\)). A
509	  morphism into the \emph{affine} \(V\) is pinned by its global-sections ring map
510	  (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), whence \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\). This realises, without
511	  cohomology, the classical ``proper connected variety into an affine is a single point'' step
512	  quoted from Mumford above.
513	\end{proof}
514	\begin{lemma}
515	\leanok
516	[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme]
517	  \label{lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract}
519	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) project-bespoke (elementary topology + algebra); NO external source.
520	  % LANDED axiom-clean as `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract`, stated generically for any
521	  % retract `r : S ⟶ T`, `pr : T ⟶ S` with `r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S` of an integral `T` (more general than
522	  % the `X`/`X×Y`/`p_1` instance below). PROOF DIVERGENCE (cosmetic, sound either way): the Lean
523	  % proves reducedness PER-STALK (each `S.stalk x` embeds via `pr.stalkMap` into the reduced
524	  % `T.stalk`, then `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`), not via the global-sections split-injection
525	  % the prose below describes. Plan/blueprint-writer may align the prose to the stalk route.
526	  Let \(X \times Y\) be an integral scheme (here it is geometrically irreducible and reduced over
527	  \(\bar k\)). Suppose \(X\) is a \emph{retract} of \(X \times Y\): there is a section
528	  \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) of the first projection \(p_1\), i.e.\ \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\).
529	  Then \(X\) is integral.
530	\end{lemma}
531	\begin{proof}
532	\leanok
533	  Integrality is reducedness together with irreducibility, and the retract supplies each.
534	  \emph{Reduced.} The section identity \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\) makes the pullback on global
535	  sections \(p_1^\sharp \colon \mathcal O_X(X) \to \mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) split
536	  injective (it is left-inverted by \(s^\sharp\)). A split-injective ring map into the reduced ring
537	  \(\mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) embeds \(\mathcal O_X(X)\) into a reduced ring, so it too is
538	  reduced (\(\mathtt{isReduced\_of\_injective}\) on the split injection); hence \(X\) is reduced.
539	  \emph{Irreducible.} The first projection \(p_1 \colon X \times Y \to X\) is surjective because it
540	  admits the section \(s\) (every point of \(X\) is \(p_1(s(\cdot))\)). The continuous image of an
541	  irreducible space under a surjection is irreducible; as \(X \times Y\) is irreducible and \(p_1\) is a
542	  continuous surjection onto \(X\), the space of \(X\) is irreducible.
543	  Reduced and irreducible together give that \(X\) is integral. This is elementary --- no cohomology,
544	  no external citation --- and feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly below, where the proper slice
545	  \(X_y \cong X\) must be presented as proper \emph{integral}.
546	\end{proof}
547	\begin{lemma}\leanok
548	[Step~1: per-closed-slice constancy of a proper slice into an affine]
549	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}
550	  
551	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}
552	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
553	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
554	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
555	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
556	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
557	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
558	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
559	  With the data of \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} --- \(\bar k\) algebraically
560	  closed, \(X\) proper over \(\bar k\), \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced, and locally of
561	  finite type, \(Z\) separated, a \(\bar k\)-point \(x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X\), a morphism
562	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\), a \(p_2\)-saturated open \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\) mapping into an
563	  affine open \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) --- fix a \emph{closed} point \(x\) of \(U\). Then \(f\) and the collapsed
564	  map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) (with $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
565	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\(, i.e.\ \)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\() agree at \)x$
566	  after the residue-field probe \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to U\):
567	  \[ U.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi (U.\iota \fatsemi f)
568	     = U.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi (U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)). \]
569	\end{lemma}
570	\begin{proof}
571	  \leanok
572	  This is Mumford's per-slice step, realised \emph{cohomology-free} (route~B): the relative Stein /
573	  proper-pushforward \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap and is
574	  deliberately avoided. The closed point \(x\) of \(U\) lies over a closed point
575	  \(y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}\); since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(Y\) is of finite type
576	  over \(\bar k\), the residue field is \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\). Saturation \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\)
577	  puts the \emph{entire} proper integral fibre \(X_y \cong X\) over \(y\) inside \(U\), so \(f\) maps \(X_y\)
578	  into the affine \(U_0\). Because \(X_y\) is proper integral over \(\bar k\), its global sections form a
579	  field (\(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\)), module-finite over \(\bar k\)
580	  (\(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\)); algebraic closedness collapses the finite

14	For the Lane B (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`) iter-188 (III.c)
15	separated-locus structural setup, the substantive residual is a pair-
16	morphism factorization through the diagonal closed immersion. The
17	blueprint cites `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` as the
18	substrate; iter-188 verified this is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227. Two
19	questions:

22	   immersion iff topological range subset" under any reasonable
23	   reduced-source hypothesis?
24	2. What is the cleanest project-side construction (with LOC), and what
25	   blocks the tensor-reducedness gap that also obstructs the other two
26	   off-target sorries (`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`)?

45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substantive substrate)
48	
49	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE at b80f227. The only `lift` variant shipped
50	  is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` with the kernel-inequality form
51	  (`f.ker ≤ g.ker`), at
52	  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:200-214`.
53	  The `lift_iff_range_subset` variant — equating factor-through with
54	  set-theoretic range containment under a reduced-source hypothesis —
55	  is not in Mathlib (verified iter-188 via `lean_leansearch` and
56	  re-verified iter-189 via `lean_leanfinder`).
57	
58	- **Mathlib substrate that DOES exist** (and which a project-side build
59	  would chain): the iter-189 search recovered the complete chain of
60	  primitives needed:
61	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`
62	    (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`):
63	    ideal-sheaf kernel of a scheme morphism.
64	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`
65	    (same file, `:698-734`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
66	    `f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom` on each affine open.
67	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.support_ker`
68	    (same file, `:839-878`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
69	    `f.ker.support = closure (Set.range f)`.
70	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.range_subset_ker_support`
71	    (same file, `:754-764`): the unconditional inclusion
72	    `Set.range f ⊆ f.ker.support`.
73	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`
74	    (same file, `:631-639`):
75	    `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical` — the Galois-connection
76	    pinning of "support determines radical".
77	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`
78	    (same file, `:622-625`):
79	    `GaloisConnection (support ·) (vanishingIdeal ·)` (with the
80	    `Closedsᵒᵈ` ordering).
81	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι`
82	    (`IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean`):
83	    `Set.range I.subschemeι.base = I.support` — for a closed immersion
84	    `i`, `Set.range i.base = i.ker.support`.
85	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift`,
86	    `lift_fac`, `lift_fac_assoc`
87	    (`ClosedImmersion.lean:206-214`): the actual lift,
88	    keyed on `f.ker ≤ g.ker`.
89	
90	- **Project's current path**: iter-188 lands a structural setup that
91	  introduces `s_pair := pullback.lift (chart0_via_fst) (chart1_via_snd) hPLB_agree`
92	  and the closed-immersion diagonal `hΔ`, leaving a single direct
93	  `sorry` at line 553 where the (III.c) recipe step 3 needs the
94	  factorization `∃ s, s ≫ Δ = s_pair`. The blueprint cites
95	  `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` to reduce this to the
96	  set-level containment of the cocycle's image in `Set.range Δ.base`.
97	
98	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (the Mathlib idiom doesn't exist),
99	  but the gap is BUILDABLE from existing Mathlib primitives.
100	
101	- **Cost of divergence**: Building `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
102	  project-side costs ~40-60 LOC; the alternative (waiting on Mathlib
103	  upstream PR) is unbounded in time.

108	### Decision 2: Tensor reducedness of `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)`
109	
110	This is the SECOND substrate needed, blocking three sorries
111	simultaneously (the cocycle factorization plus the two off-target
112	`gm_geomIrred` / `projGm_isReduced` instances at L755, L779).
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom check**: NONE direct. The closest shipped:
115	  - `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` class
116	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean:47`):
117	    requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗_k A)`. The bridge
118	    "k alg-closed ⟹ IsReduced ⟹ IsGeometricallyReduced k" is NOT
119	    shipped (the file's own TODO confirms: "for every field extension
120	    K of k the K-algebra K ⊗[k] A is reduced" — not landed for the
121	    general field-extension case).
122	  - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
123	    (same file, `:52`): an instance for `IsReduced (K ⊗_k A)` when
124	    `K/k` algebraic AND `A` geometrically reduced over `k`. Doesn't
125	    apply directly because neither factor here is algebraic over kbar.
126	  - `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective`
127	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean`): the abstract route
128	    "tensor of two domains embedding into a common domain with
129	    linearly-disjoint ranges is a domain". Building the LinearDisjoint
130	    witness for our concrete factors would be its own ~30-50 LOC
131	    sub-task.
132	
133	- **The clean project path** (the analogist's recommended route):
134	  exploit Mathlib's **localization–polynomial tensor product
135	  iso machinery**. The relevant Mathlib lemmas, both shipped:
136	  - `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
137	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`):
138	    `TensorProduct R A S ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away ((algebraMap R S) r)`
139	    when `A = Localization.Away r` over `R`. Tensor of a localization
140	    with anything is a localization (in the tensored ring).
141	  - `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`
142	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MvPolynomial.lean`):
143	    `TensorProduct R A (MvPolynomial σ R) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial σ A`.
144	    Tensor with a polynomial ring over the base is just polynomials
145	    over the algebra.
146	
147	  Both factors of our tensor are localizations of polynomial rings
148	  over kbar:
149	  - `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`
150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.
151	  - `Away (X_0·X_1) = HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projLineBarGrading) (X_0·X_1)`
152	    — a kbar-algebra that is a domain (already proven in
153	    `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` chain via the `val_injective`
154	    embedding into `Localization.Away (X_0·X_1)`).
155	
156	  Composition:
157	  1. `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar kbar[t] ≃ MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))`
158	     via `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`.
159	  2. The localization `kbar[t] → kbar[t][1/t] = GmRing` corresponds via
160	     `IsScalarTower` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv` to
161	     the localization at the same generator's image in the tensored
162	     polynomial ring.
163	  3. Result: `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing ≃ Localization.Away (X () in MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1)))`.
164	  4. `MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))` is a polynomial ring over a
165	     domain, hence a domain.
166	  5. Localization at powers of `X ()` (a non-zero divisor in the
167	     polynomial ring over a domain) preserves the domain property
168	     via `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` +
169	     `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors`
170	     + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` (already in the project's playbook,
171	     used in `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` L734-738).
172	
173	  Therefore `IsDomain (Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing)` follows, and
174	  `IsReduced` follows from `IsDomain` directly.
175	
176	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — buildable at ~50-80 LOC.
177	  No new Mathlib infrastructure needed; everything is a chain of
178	  existing iso lemmas. The same pattern unifies the `gm_geomIrred` /
179	  `projGm_isReduced` closures (same generators, different chart degrees).
180

255	## Concrete project-side recipe
256	
257	### Substrate 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
258	
259	Target signature:
260	
261	```lean
262	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset
263	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
264	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
265	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔
266	      Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base
267	```
268	
269	Body skeleton (with `≈ 40-60` LOC):
270	
271	```lean
272	  constructor
273	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: trivial range pushdown.
274	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
275	    intro x ⟨y, rfl⟩
276	    exact ⟨h.base y, rfl⟩
277	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substance.
278	    intro hrange
279	    -- Step 1: closed immersion ⟹ range is closed, equal to ker.support.
280	    have hi_range : Set.range i.base = (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
281	      rw [← IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι]
282	      -- Use that i = subschemeι i.ker (up to iso) via toImage_imageι
283	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC bridge using Scheme.Hom.toImage / imageι
284	    -- Step 2: g.ker.support = closure (range g) ⊆ i.ker.support
285	    --         (latter is closed).
286	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := by
287	      rw [Hom.support_ker]
288	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.isClosed_supportSet
289	      exact hrange.trans hi_range.le
290	    -- Step 3: Y reduced ⟹ g.ker is radical
291	    --         (since g.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (g.app U)
292	    --          and Y reduced ⟹ that kernel is radical).
293	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
294	      ext U : 2
295	      simp only [IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, g.ker_apply]
296	      exact (RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective
297	        Function.Surjective.rfl).mpr inferInstance  -- needs adjustment
298	      -- Concretely: use `f.app U`'s kernel-is-radical because Y is reduced;
299	      -- the appropriate lemma is `RingHom.ker_isRadical_of_reduced_codomain`.
300	      sorry  -- ~10 LOC; standard "reduced source ⟹ kernel of ring map radical"
301	    -- Step 4: Z reduced ⟹ i.ker is radical (same argument).
302	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
303	      ext U : 2
304	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC, symmetric to Step 3
305	    -- Step 5: combine via Galois connection.
306	    --         g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support
307	    --         ⟹ vanishingIdeal(i.ker.support) ≤ vanishingIdeal(g.ker.support)
308	    --         ⟹ i.ker.radical ≤ g.ker.radical (by `vanishingIdeal_support`)
309	    --         ⟹ i.ker ≤ g.ker (by Steps 3, 4).
310	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
311	      calc i.ker
312	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
313	        _ = vanishingIdeal i.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_support.symm
314	        _ ≤ vanishingIdeal g.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
315	        _ = g.ker.radical := vanishingIdeal_support
316	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
317	    -- Step 6: apply Mathlib's IsClosedImmersion.lift.
318	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker,
319	           IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
320	```
321	
322	**Notes on the LOC**:
323	- The two "kernel-is-radical" steps (3, 4) might need a Mathlib bridge
324	  lemma not directly searched here; if so, that adds ~10-15 LOC for an
325	  inline derivation (use `Hom.ker_apply` to drop to ring kernels, then
326	  reducedness of the source gives radicality of the ring kernel — a
327	  standard `RingHom.ker_isRadical` argument).
328	- The Step 1 bridge (closed immersion `i` is the `subschemeι` of its
329	  kernel, up to iso) uses `Scheme.Hom.toImage_imageι` from Mathlib.
330	
331	**Total**: ~40-60 LOC.
332	
333	### Substrate 2: tensor reducedness `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar GmRing`
334	
335	Target signature (generic in the homogeneous generator `f` of degree `d`):
336	
337	```lean
338	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
339	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d)
340	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
341	    (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
342	    (hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
343	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
344	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
345	      (GmRing kbar))
346	```
347	
348	Body skeleton (with `≈ 50-80` LOC):
349	
350	```lean
351	  -- Step 1: Away 𝒜 f is a domain (project-side, used in
352	  -- projectiveLineBar_isReduced L734-738).
353	  haveI hf_dom : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
354	      (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) := by
355	    refine Function.Injective.isDomain
356	      (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ f) (Localization.Away f)) ?_
357	    · intro x y h
358	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
359	  -- Step 2: Build the iso chain.
360	  -- (a) MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv :
361	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[Away 𝒜 f]
362	  --       MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f)
363	  -- (b) IsScalarTower over kbar lifts the localization at X () in MvPoly Unit kbar
364	  --     to the corresponding localization at X () in MvPoly Unit (Away 𝒜 f).
365	  -- (c) Resulting iso:
366	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (GmRing kbar) ≃
367	  --       Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f))
368	  -- Step 3: RHS is a domain.
369	  -- MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f) is a polynomial ring over a domain,
370	  -- hence a domain (instance auto-resolves).
371	  -- Localization at powers of X () (a non-zero divisor, by
372	  -- MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero) preserves the domain property via
373	  -- IsLocalization.isDomain_localization + powers_le_nonZeroDivisors.
374	  sorry  -- ~50-80 LOC threading the iso chain and applying domain instances
375	```
376	
377	The body's main work is threading `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
378	(or its symmetric `tensorEquiv` variant) at the `kbar[t] → GmRing`
379	localization step, after collapsing the `Away 𝒜 f ⊗_kbar kbar[t]` tensor
380	via `algebraTensorAlgEquiv`. The threading requires careful instance
381	management (`IsScalarTower kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f))`,
382	the `Algebra` from polynomial ring over an algebra, the `IsLocalization.Away`
383	instance on the tensored side) — accounting for the upper end of the
384	50-80 LOC range.
385	
386	**Corollary**:
387	```lean
388	instance : IsReduced (Spec (.of (TensorProduct kbar (Away _ f) (GmRing kbar))))
389	```
390	follows in ~3 LOC via `inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec ...))` after the
391	domain instance lands.
392	
393	### Application 1: cocycle body completion
394	
395	The iter-188 structural setup already lands the harness in
396	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`:
397	- `s_pair`, `hs_fst`, `hs_snd`, `hΔ`, `hsep`, `hPLB_agree` are all built.
398	- Goal at line 553 is reducible to `∃ s, s ≫ Δ = s_pair`.
399	
400	With Substrates 1+2 in hand:
401	1. Establish `IsReduced (intersection)` by transporting the `Spec` domain
402	   instance across `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` (~10 LOC).
403	2. Establish the range containment `range s_pair.base ⊆ range Δ.base`
404	   by closed-points check (each closed point of intersection lies over
405	   a single kbar-point of `D_+(X_0·X_1) × Gm`, both chart-maps evaluate
406	   to the same PLB-point) — ~15-25 LOC via point-checking on closed points
407	   (kbar-rational since kbar is alg-closed and intersection is locally
408	   of finite type).
409	3. Apply `lift_iff_range_subset` to extract `s`, then close via
410	   `pullback.diagonal_fst` / `_snd` (= 𝟙) — ~10 LOC.
411	
412	**Total application**: ~35-50 LOC.
413	
414	### Application 2: bonus sorries
415	
416	The same Substrate 2 closes:
417	- `projGm_isReduced` (~15-25 LOC): cover `(PLB ⊗ Gm).left` by the
418	  per-chart `Spec (Away X_i ⊗ GmRing)` (using the existing
419	  `gmScalingP1_cover`), each reduced by Substrate 2 at the degree-1
420	  generator; conclude via `IsReduced.of_openCover`.
421	- `gm_geomIrred` (~15-30 LOC): for `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, every algebraic
422	  extension K/kbar is isomorphic to kbar (up to alg-closure equivalence),
423	  so the base-change `GmRing ⊗_kbar K = Localization.Away t in K[t]`
424	  is a domain → `Spec` is irreducible. The Mathlib bridge
425	  `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
426	  (line 52 of `GeometricallyReduced.lean`) MIGHT apply once
427	  `GmRing kbar` is shown geometrically reduced — that's a separate
428	  ~10-15 LOC argument.
429	
430	**Total bonus**: ~30-55 LOC for both.
431	
432	## Total LOC estimate
433	
434	| Sub-task | LOC | Substrate or application? |
435	|---|---|---|
436	| Substrate 1: `lift_iff_range_subset` | 40–60 | substrate (Cross01Substrate.lean) |
437	| Substrate 2: tensor reducedness | 50–80 | substrate (Cross01Substrate.lean) |
438	| Application: cocycle body | 35–50 | inline in GmScaling.lean |
439	| Bonus: `projGm_isReduced` + `gm_geomIrred` | 30–55 | inline in GmScaling.lean |
440	
441	**Total**: ~155–245 LOC over 3–5 iters.
442	
443	This matches the iter-188 escalation's ~150-200 LOC estimate at the
444	lower end and slightly exceeds at the upper end. The substrate work is
445	front-loaded (Substrates 1+2 = ~90-140 LOC in iters 189-190); the
446	application work is rapid follow-on (iters 191-193).
447	
448	## Hard binary verdict
449	
450	**(B) FEASIBLE at 80-200 LOC.**
451	
452	The substrate (Substrate 1 + Substrate 2 combined) lands at
453	~90-140 LOC; the cocycle application closes within 35-50 LOC; the
454	bonus sorries close within 30-55 LOC. Total ranges 155-245 LOC, with
455	the central estimate at ~180-200 LOC.
456	
457	**Verdict A (FEASIBLE at <80 LOC) is ruled out**: Mathlib does not
458	ship `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` and there is no
459	shorter Mathlib-direct route. The substrate is genuine work, not a
460	naming-only gap.
461	
462	**Verdict C (NOT FEASIBLE at <200 LOC, USER ESCALATION) is NOT triggered**:
463	the work is bounded and uses only existing Mathlib primitives. No
464	upstream Mathlib PR is required. The Galois-connection chain
465	(`vanishingIdeal ↔ support` with `Hom.support_ker` and
466	`range_subschemeι`) is the canonical Stacks-style path and is fully
467	formalized in Mathlib.
468	
469	## Recommendation
470	
471	Proceed with **Option B (project-side substrate)** as planned. Land
472	the work in a new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`
473	containing the two substrate lemmas, imported by `GmScaling.lean`.
474	
475	**Phased delivery (3-5 iters)**:
476	- **Iter 189**: dispatch a prover on `Cross01Substrate.lean` to land
477	  Substrate 1 (`lift_iff_range_subset`); this is the smaller chunk
478	  (~40-60 LOC) and unblocks the application path independently.
479	- **Iter 190**: dispatch a prover on `Cross01Substrate.lean` to land
480	  Substrate 2 (tensor reducedness); this is the larger chunk
481	  (~50-80 LOC) and uses Mathlib's iso-chain machinery
482	  (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`).
483	- **Iter 191-193**: dispatch a prover on `GmScaling.lean` to close
484	  the three open sorries (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`,
485	  `gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) using both substrates.
486	
487	**Reversal trigger**: if iter-190's tensor reducedness substrate
488	fails to type-check at the `tensorRightEquiv` step due to a
489	`Algebra kbar ...` / `IsScalarTower` instance mismatch, fall back to
490	the `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective` route (build
491	the linear-disjoint witness via the polynomial-ring-disjoint-variables
492	classical fact). Same LOC budget; different elaboration shape.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Points
9	
10	/-!
11	# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas
12	
13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
16	(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.
17	
18	The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
19	file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced
29	  source / target hypotheses).
30	
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` —
32	  Substrate 2 (iter-190): the tensor
33	  `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain, via the iso chain to
34	  `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` per
35	  `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3.
36	-/
37	
38	set_option autoImplicit false
39	set_option linter.style.setOption false
40	set_option linter.style.show false
41	set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false
42	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
43	
44	universe u
45	
46	open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology
47	
48	noncomputable section
49	
50	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
51	
52	namespace IsClosedImmersion
53	
54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.
60	
61	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
62	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
63	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
64	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
65	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
66	
67	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
68	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
69	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
70	-/
71	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
72	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
73	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
74	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
75	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
76	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
77	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
78	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
79	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
80	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
81	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
82	    intro hrange
83	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
84	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
85	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
86	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
87	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
88	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
89	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
90	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
91	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
92	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
93	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
94	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
95	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
96	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
97	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
98	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
99	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
100	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
101	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
102	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
103	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
104	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
105	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
106	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
112	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
113	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
114	      intro U
115	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
116	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
117	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
118	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
119	      calc i.ker
120	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
121	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
122	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
123	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
124	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
125	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
126	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
127	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
128	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
129	
130	end IsClosedImmersion
131	
132	/--
133	**Substrate 2 — `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain.**
134	
135	For a non-zero homogeneous element `f` of positive degree `d` in the
136	`projectiveLineBar` grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, the tensor
137	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄`
138	is an integral domain.
139	
140	The proof identifies the tensor with the localization
141	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` via the
142	chain of natural isomorphisms in `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3:
143	* `Away f ⊗_{k̄} 𝕜[t] ≃ 𝕜[t][Away f]` (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`),
144	* tensor base change with the localization
145	  `𝕜[t] → GmRing k̄` produces `Localization.Away (X ())`
146	  (`IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight`).
147	
148	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` is a domain because
149	it is the localization of `MvPolynomial Unit (Away f)` (a polynomial ring
150	over the domain `Away f`) at the powers of the non-zero generator `X ()`.
151	
152	This is **Substrate 2** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
153	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
154	`Theorem~\ref{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}`.
155	-/
156	@[nolint unusedArguments]
157	theorem gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
158	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (_hd : 0 < d)
159	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
160	    (_hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
161	    (_hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
162	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
163	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
164	      (GmRing kbar)) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- Abbreviation for the homogeneous localization (a `kbar`-algebra and a domain).
167	  set A : Type u := HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f
168	  -- =====================================================================
169	  -- Step 1: `A` is a domain.
170	  -- =====================================================================
171	  -- The bridge `val : A → Localization.Away f` is injective, and we transport
172	  -- domain-ness via `Function.Injective.isDomain`.  This pattern is the same
173	  -- one used inside `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` (`GmScaling.lean` L734-738).
174	  haveI hLocAway_dom :
175	      IsDomain (Localization.Away (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) := by
176	    have hPoly_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) := inferInstance
177	    by_cases hf_zero : f = 0
178	    · -- if f = 0, Localization.Away f is Subsingleton, but we still need IsDomain.
179	      -- Instead, contradict with hf_ne.
180	      exact absurd hf_zero _hf_ne
181	    · exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
182	        (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_zero)
183	  haveI hA_dom : IsDomain A :=
184	    Function.Injective.isDomain (algebraMap A (Localization.Away f))
185	      (fun x y h => HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h)
186	  -- =====================================================================
187	  -- Step 2: `MvPolynomial Unit A` is a domain (polynomial ring over a domain).
188	  -- =====================================================================
189	  haveI hT_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit A) := inferInstance
190	  -- =====================================================================
191	  -- Step 3: Build the natural iso
192	  --   `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) ≃ₐ[A] Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`.
193	  -- =====================================================================
194	  -- Step 3a:  `A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A`.
195	  -- This is `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` directly.
196	  let e1 : TensorProduct kbar A (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A :=
197	    MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv kbar A
198	  -- Step 3b: Establish IsLocalization of the target tensor.
199	  -- We use the `Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` to view the tensor as a
200	  -- localization. The plan is to set up the algebra structure correctly
201	  -- so that `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight` applies.
202	  --
203	  -- Plan: build a `RingEquiv` from `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` to
204	  -- `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`, then transport IsDomain.
205	  --
206	  -- For the iso, we use:
207	  --   `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) ≃ A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) localized at X()`
208	  --   ≃ `MvPolynomial Unit A` localized at X () (via e1)
209	  --   ≃ `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`.
210	  --
211	  -- The first iso uses that tensor commutes with localization.
212	  -- We package this via `IsLocalization` instances + uniqueness of localization.
213	  --
214	  -- Concretely, we apply `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` once we
215	  -- establish that `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` is the localization of a domain
216	  -- at a non-zero-divisor submonoid.
217	  --
218	  -- The cleanest path: show `IsLocalization (powers (1 ⊗ X())) (A ⊗[kbar] Gm)`,
219	  -- where the base ring `S := A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` is a domain
220	  -- (via `e1`), and `1 ⊗ X()` is non-zero (corresponding to `X ()` in
221	  -- `MvPolynomial Unit A` via `e1`).
222	  set S : Type u := TensorProduct kbar A (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
223	  haveI hS_dom : IsDomain S :=
224	    Function.Injective.isDomain e1.toRingHom e1.injective
225	  -- The element we localize at: `1 ⊗ X () : S`.
226	  let xS : S := (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
227	  -- `xS ≠ 0`: it maps under `e1` to `X () : MvPolynomial Unit A`, which is non-zero.
228	  have hxS_ne : xS ≠ 0 := by
229	    intro h
230	    have he1 : e1 xS = e1 0 := by rw [h]
231	    have he1' : e1 xS = (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit A) := by
232	      simp [e1, xS, MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv_tmul]
233	    rw [he1'] at he1
234	    simp at he1
235	  -- Now establish: `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` is an `IsLocalization` of `S` at `powers xS`.
236	  -- We use `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight` with a tweak.
237	  --
238	  -- Detail: set up the algebra structures so that S = A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
239	  -- is a `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-algebra via `rightAlgebra`, and then
240	  -- `(GmRing kbar) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] S` is a localization of `S`
241	  -- at the image of `powers (X())`, which equals `powers xS`.
242	  --
243	  -- Then identify `(GmRing kbar) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] S ≃ A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)`
244	  -- via base-change cancellation.
245	  --
246	  -- To avoid heavy elaboration, we construct the iso explicitly by
247	  -- universal properties.
248	  letI rightAlg : Algebra (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) S :=
249	    Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
250	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) S := inferInstance
251	  -- A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) has commuting algebra maps from A and Gm.
252	  -- Define forward: φ : A ⊗[kbar] Gm →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS
253	  -- via TensorProduct.lift on (A → LocAwayxS) and (Gm → LocAwayxS).
254	  -- (b) Gm → LocAwayxS via IsLocalization.Away.lift, using that X () maps to
255	  --     a unit in Localization.Away xS.
256	  -- The base map MvPolynomial Unit kbar → Localization.Away xS sends X () to
257	  -- the image of `xS` in `Localization.Away xS`, which is a unit.
258	  let polkToLocAwayxS : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
259	    (Algebra.ofId S (Localization.Away xS)).restrictScalars kbar |>.comp
260	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight :
261	        MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] S)
262	  -- Image of X () under polkToLocAwayxS is the image of xS in LocAwayxS.
263	  have hX_unit_polk : IsUnit
264	      (polkToLocAwayxS (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
265	    have h_eq : polkToLocAwayxS (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
266	        = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) xS := by
267	      show (Algebra.ofId S (Localization.Away xS)).toRingHom
268	        ((Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] S)
269	          (MvPolynomial.X ())) = _
270	      rfl
271	    rw [h_eq]
272	    exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit xS
273	  let f2_ring : GmRing kbar →+* Localization.Away xS :=
274	    IsLocalization.Away.lift
275	      (g := polkToLocAwayxS.toRingHom)
276	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hX_unit_polk
277	  -- Promote to AlgHom over kbar.
278	  let f2 : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
279	    { f2_ring with
280	      commutes' := fun k => by
281	        show f2_ring _ = _
282	        have step :
283	            (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) k
284	              = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))
285	                ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) k) :=
286	          (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
287	            (GmRing kbar) k).symm
288	        rw [step]
289	        show f2_ring ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) _) = _
290	        rw [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq]
291	        exact polkToLocAwayxS.commutes k }
292	  -- Promote f1 to AlgHom kbar via restrictScalars.
293	  let f1_kbar : A →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
294	    (Algebra.ofId A (Localization.Away xS)).restrictScalars kbar
295	  -- Build φ.
296	  let φ : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar) →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
297	    Algebra.TensorProduct.lift f1_kbar f2 (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)
298	  -- =====================================================================
299	  -- Step 4: Build ψ : Localization.Away xS →+* TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)
300	  -- =====================================================================
301	  -- First the map S = A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) → target.
302	  -- We use Algebra.TensorProduct.map (AlgHom.id A) (algebraMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar → Gm).
303	  let target : Type u := TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)
304	  let s_to_target_alg : S →ₐ[kbar] target :=
305	    Algebra.TensorProduct.map (AlgHom.id kbar A)
306	      (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))
307	  let s_to_target : S →+* target := s_to_target_alg.toRingHom
308	  -- Image of xS is `1 ⊗ X()` (where X () is mapped to GmRing kbar), which is a unit.
309	  have hxS_unit_target : IsUnit (s_to_target xS) := by
310	    have h_eq : s_to_target xS = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
311	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
312	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
313	      show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = _
314	      simp [s_to_target_alg, Algebra.TensorProduct.map_tmul]
315	      rfl
316	    rw [h_eq]
317	    -- 1 ⊗ (unit element) is a unit. Use the inverse 1 ⊗ (inv element).
318	    refine IsUnit.map
319	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target).toRingHom ?_
320	    exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
321	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
322	  -- Lift to ψ.
323	  let ψ_ring : Localization.Away xS →+* target :=
324	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (g := s_to_target) xS hxS_unit_target
325	  -- =====================================================================
326	  -- Step 5: Show φ ∘ ψ_ring = id  and  ψ_ring ∘ φ = id on generators.
327	  -- For Step 5, we only need ψ_ring ∘ φ = id (left inverse → injective);
328	  -- then `IsDomain` transfers via `Function.Injective.isDomain` + LocAwayxS domain.
329	  -- =====================================================================
330	  -- First establish that LocAwayxS is a domain.
331	  haveI hLocAwayxS_dom : IsDomain (Localization.Away xS) :=
332	    IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors _
333	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hxS_ne)
334	  -- =====================================================================
335	  -- Step 5: Show ψ_ring ∘ φ = id (left inverse → φ injective → IsDomain).
336	  -- =====================================================================
337	  -- Helper A: ψ_ring on the image of algebraMap S → Localization.Away xS is s_to_target.
338	  have hψ_alg :
339	      ∀ a : S, ψ_ring ((algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) a) = s_to_target a := by
340	    intro a
341	    show IsLocalization.Away.lift xS hxS_unit_target
342	        ((algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) a) = _
343	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq xS hxS_unit_target a
344	  -- Helper B: ψ_ring (f1_kbar a) = a ⊗ 1.
345	  have h_psi_f1 : ∀ a : A, ψ_ring (f1_kbar a) = (a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : GmRing kbar) := by
346	    intro a
347	    have heq : f1_kbar a = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS))
348	        ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
349	      show (algebraMap A (Localization.Away xS)) a = _
350	      rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A S (Localization.Away xS) a]
351	      rfl
352	    rw [heq, hψ_alg]
353	    show s_to_target_alg ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = _
354	    rw [show s_to_target_alg ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
355	        = (AlgHom.id kbar A) a ⊗ₜ[kbar]
356	          (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) 1 from rfl]
357	    simp
358	  -- Helper C: ψ_ring (f2 b) = 1 ⊗ b. We show ψ_ring ∘ f2 = includeRight via
359	  -- the universal property of `IsLocalization.Away (X()) (GmRing kbar)`.
360	  have h_psi_f2_aux : ∀ p : MvPolynomial Unit kbar,
361	      ψ_ring (f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p))
362	        = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
363	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) p) := by
364	    intro p
365	    -- f2 (algebraMap _ _ p) = polkToLocAwayxS p
366	    have step1 : f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p)
367	        = polkToLocAwayxS p := by
368	      show f2_ring _ = _
369	      exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
370	        hX_unit_polk p
371	    rw [step1]
372	    -- polkToLocAwayxS p = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ p)
373	    have step2 : polkToLocAwayxS p
374	        = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS))
375	            ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p) := rfl
376	    rw [step2, hψ_alg]
377	    -- s_to_target ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ p) = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ algebraMap _ _ p
378	    show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p) = _
379	    rw [show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p)
380	        = (AlgHom.id kbar A) 1 ⊗ₜ[kbar]
381	          (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p from rfl]
382	    simp
383	  -- Helper D: ψ_ring (f2 b) = 1 ⊗ b. Two ring homs `GmRing kbar →+* target` agree iff
384	  -- they agree after composing with `algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)`.
385	  have h_psi_f2 : ∀ b : GmRing kbar,
386	      ψ_ring (f2 b) = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (b : GmRing kbar) := by
387	    have h_eq_ringhoms : (ψ_ring.comp f2.toRingHom)
388	        = ((Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight :
389	            GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target).toRingHom) := by
390	      refine IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers
391	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) ?_
392	      refine RingHom.ext fun p => ?_
393	      show ψ_ring (f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p))
394	        = (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target)
395	            ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p)
396	      rw [h_psi_f2_aux p]
397	      rfl
398	    intro b
399	    have hb := RingHom.congr_fun h_eq_ringhoms b
400	    -- hb : ψ_ring (f2 b) = includeRight b = 1 ⊗ b
401	    exact hb
402	  -- Main: ψ_ring ∘ φ = id on every tensor element.
403	  have h_left_inv : ∀ x : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar), ψ_ring (φ x) = x := by
404	    intro x
405	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
406	    | zero =>
407	      have hφ0 : φ (0 : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)) = 0 := φ.map_zero
408	      rw [hφ0, ψ_ring.map_zero]
409	    | tmul a b =>
410	      have h_phi : φ (a ⊗ₜ b) = f1_kbar a * f2 b := by
411	        show Algebra.TensorProduct.lift f1_kbar f2 _ (a ⊗ₜ b) = _
412	        rw [Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul]
413	      rw [h_phi]
414	      rw [ψ_ring.map_mul]
415	      rw [h_psi_f1 a, h_psi_f2 b]
416	      rw [Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul]
417	      rw [mul_one, one_mul]
418	    | add x y hx hy =>
419	      have hφxy : φ (x + y) = φ x + φ y := φ.map_add _ _
420	      rw [hφxy, ψ_ring.map_add, hx, hy]
421	  -- Conclude IsDomain via φ injective.
422	  refine Function.Injective.isDomain φ.toRingHom ?_
423	  intro x y hxy
424	  have h1 : ψ_ring (φ x) = ψ_ring (φ y) := by
425	    show ψ_ring (φ.toRingHom x) = ψ_ring (φ.toRingHom y)
426	    rw [hxy]
427	  rw [h_left_inv x, h_left_inv y] at h1
428	  exact h1

150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance

240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
